sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.15 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: For an automorphism$Aut(G)$of a group$G$, let$[e]_{ } =\{g^{-1}g^{ } g G\}$. Is it true that if a group$G$has trivial center, then there is an inner automorphism$$such that$[e]_{ }$is not a subgroup? { V.\,G.\,Bardakov, M.\,V.\,Neshchadim, T.\,R.\,Nasybullov} No, not always; see V.\,V.\,Bludov's Example~2 in (D.\,Gon alves, T.\,Nasybullov, Commun. Algebra, { 47}, no.\,3 (2019), 930--944).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
